page: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-16g-4vram is a virtual machine within the Cloud GPU family, featuring a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU. The instance is configured with 0.25 of a physical GPU, providing 4 GB of dedicated VRAM. It is powered by an x86_64 Intel Core Processor based on the Broadwell architecture, running at 2.0 GHz with 2 shared vCPUs and 2 physical cores. System memory consists of 16 GB RAM, yielding 8 GB per core. Local storage is provided by an 80 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear contrast between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. The shared Intel CPU delivers modest performance, with stress-ng single-core results in the average tier and multi-core performance in the weak tier. Memory bandwidth is also relatively low, with uncached large-block reads at 28.99 GB/sec and writes at 21.41 GB/sec. However, the fractional NVIDIA Ampere GPU excels at lightweight machine learning tasks. LLM inference benchmarks for a small 135M model show top-tier prompt processing at 8,765.82 tokens per second and strong text generation at 296.05 tokens per second. Even with a medium 7B model, prompt processing remains top-tier at 308.92 tokens per second, though text generation drops to a weak 7.83 tokens per second.

This instance offers a highly cost-efficient entry point for GPU-accelerated computing by utilizing fractional hardware resources. By bundling a quarter of an NVIDIA A16 GPU with shared CPU cores, it avoids the high costs associated with dedicated full-GPU instances. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation and lower memory bandwidth, which makes it poorly suited for heavy database operations or high-throughput web serving. It is best suited for lightweight LLM inference, model prototyping, small-scale AI workloads, and applications requiring hardware acceleration without the need for massive CPU compute power.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-16g-4vram is a fractional GPU instance designed for budget-conscious accelerated workloads. It features 0.25 of an NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 4 GB of VRAM, paired with 2 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16 GB of system RAM and 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While CPU-bound benchmarks and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, the instance delivers top-tier prompt processing speeds for small and medium language models, reaching 8,765.82 tokens per second on a 135M model. This resource density offers a cost-effective alternative to full-GPU instances. The server is best suited for lightweight machine learning inference, AI prototyping, and workloads that benefit from GPU acceleration but do not require intensive CPU performance or high memory bandwidth.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-16g-4vram is a fractional GPU instance featuring 0.25 of an NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 4 GB VRAM, 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an 80 GB NVMe SSD for budget-friendly AI inference workloads.

tagline: A cost-effective fractional GPU instance combining an NVIDIA Ampere accelerator with shared CPU resources for lightweight machine learning and prototyping.
bullet_points: 0.25 NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 4 GB of dedicated VRAM
2 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs and 16 GB of system RAM
80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier prompt processing speeds for small and medium LLMs
Cost-effective resource profile for entry-level GPU workloads
Best suited for lightweight AI inference and model prototyping
